title: Add and Search with '.' Wildcards
description:
Given Build a data structure that supports adding lowercase words and searching where the query string may contain '.' characters that match any single letter. The design should use a trie and a depth-first search when a wildcard is encountered. Focus on writing a clean DFS that branches only when needed and terminates early when the path cannot lead to a full match.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ total characters added ≤ 2·10^6
- 0 ≤ number of searches ≤ 2·10^5
- Words are lowercase 'a'..'z'
explanation:
Intuition
Exact characters follow a single path, while '.' requires exploring all children at that position. The branching factor B is at most 26, but early pruning keeps typical queries fast.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Store words in a trie
2. Main Algorithm: On search, use DFS: if char is '.', try all children; else follow the exact child
3. Return Result: Accept only if we end at an end node after consuming all characters

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(L·B) worst case
- Space Complexity: O(total characters)

Key Insights
- Wildcards trigger branching only at those positions
- Returning early when a branch succeeds is key

Common Pitfalls
- Accepting partial paths without checking end flag
- Exploring all children even after a match is wasteful
hints: Stop early when a branch succeeds
